dataFields.proposer: actor id submitting the order and setting the window

dataFields.inWindow: boolean, true iff the approval arrives before the window end
boolean, true iff the release attempt is before the window end
boolean, true iff the execute attempt is before the window end

dataFields.windowPassed: boolean, true iff the window end has passed

specialRules.name: proposer-cannot-approve
specialRules.note: Reject an APPROVE whose approver equals the stored proposer; the proposer is never a valid authority.

specialRules.name: duplicate-approver-ignored
specialRules.note: An APPROVE whose approver equals firstApprover is a no-op; the same authority cannot count as both required approvals.

specialRules.name: approve-outside-window
specialRules.note: Reject an APPROVE with inWindow == false; late approvals never count toward the two required.

specialRules.name: second-distinct-approval-promotes
specialRules.note: Only the second distinct in-window non-proposer approval moves the order to approved; the first merely records firstApprover and stays pending.

specialRules.name: release-outside-window
specialRules.note: Reject a RELEASE with inWindow == false; transmit must never fire outside the window.

specialRules.name: release-before-approval
specialRules.note: Reject RELEASE while still pending; an order without two distinct approvals must never be transmitted.

specialRules.name: release-retry-no-retransmit
specialRules.note: A repeated RELEASE while already released is a no-op and must not fire the transmit side effect a second time (transmitted stays true, post == pre).

specialRules.name: execute-outside-window
specialRules.note: Reject an EXECUTE with inWindow == false; the external action must never fire outside the window.

specialRules.name: execute-requires-transmit
specialRules.note: Reject EXECUTE if transmitted == false, even in acknowledged state; execution without a prior transmit is a safety violation.

specialRules.name: execute-requires-dual-auth
specialRules.note: Reject EXECUTE if firstApprover == 'none' or the dual-authorization invariant is broken; defense in depth for the two-distinct-approvers rule.

specialRules.name: execute-before-ack
specialRules.note: Reject EXECUTE while merely released; acknowledgement must precede execution.

specialRules.name: execute-retry-no-repeat
specialRules.note: A repeated EXECUTE while already executed is a no-op and must not fire the external action a second time (executedOnce stays true, post == pre).

specialRules.name: no-abort-after-execute
specialRules.note: Reject ABORT once executed; recall is only allowed at any point strictly before execution.

specialRules.name: expire-requires-window-passed
specialRules.note: An EXPIRE with windowPassed == false is a no-op; expiry is only valid once the window end has actually passed. Applies identically from approved, released, and acknowledged.

specialRules.name: no-expire-after-execute
specialRules.note: An executed order never expires; EXPIRE in executed state is a no-op even with windowPassed == true.

specialRules.name: no-repropose
specialRules.note: A second PROPOSE after the order exists is a no-op; the proposer and window are fixed at first submission.

specialRules.name: terminal-closed-final
specialRules.note: A closed order is final; every action in closed yields post == pre.

specialRules.name: terminal-aborted-final
specialRules.note: An aborted order is final; every action in aborted yields post == pre.

specialRules.name: terminal-expired-final
specialRules.note: An expired order is final; every action in expired yields post == pre.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply in the current state yields post == pre.
